questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film Pyaasa released?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa was released in 1957.

questions.q: Who directed the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa was directed by Guru Dutt, who also played the lead role.

questions.q: Who played the lead role of the struggling poet Vijay in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt himself played the disillusioned poet Vijay in Pyaasa.

questions.q: What is the profession of the central character Vijay in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Vijay is a struggling, neglected poet whose work is ignored until he is presumed dead.

questions.q: Which lyricist wrote the songs for the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Sahir Ludhianvi penned the lyrics for Pyaasa, including its socially critical songs.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman composed the score and songs for Pyaasa.

questions.q: Which actress played the kind-hearted prostitute Gulabo in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Waheeda Rehman played Gulabo, the prostitute who believes in Vijay's poetry.

questions.q: Which actress played Meena, Vijay's former love who marries a publisher, in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Mala Sinha played Meena, the woman who left Vijay to marry the wealthy publisher Mr. Ghosh.

questions.q: Which comic actor played Abdul Sattar, the masseur, in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Johnny Walker played the masseur Abdul Sattar and performed the song 'Sar Jo Tera Chakraye'.

questions.q: Which song from the 1957 film Pyaasa is a biting critique of post-Independence Indian society and its treatment of the poor?
questions.explanation: 'Jinhe Naaz Hai Hind Par' is the song in which Vijay condemns the exploitation in newly independent India.

questions.q: Which playback singer voiced most of Vijay's songs, including 'Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaye', in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi sang the key songs of Vijay, the poet played by Guru Dutt.

questions.q: Which haunting song does Vijay sing as he denounces the so-called civilized world at the climax of the 1957 film Pyaasa?
options: Aaj Sajan Mohe Ang Laga Lo
Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaye To Kya Hai
questions.explanation: 'Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaye To Kya Hai' is the song in which Vijay rejects a hypocritical world.

questions.q: In the 1957 film Pyaasa, society begins to value the poet Vijay only after which event?
questions.explanation: Vijay's poetry is celebrated only when he is mistakenly believed to be dead.

questions.q: In which language was Guru Dutt's 1957 film Pyaasa made?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa was made in Hindi-Urdu (Hindustani), the standard language of mainstream Bombay cinema.

questions.q: Who was the cinematographer responsible for the celebrated black-and-white visuals of the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: V. K. Murthy was the cinematographer whose lighting defined the look of Pyaasa.

questions.q: What is the meaning of the title of Guru Dutt's 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: 'Pyaasa' translates to 'thirsty' or 'the thirsty one', reflecting the poet's longing.

questions.q: Under which banner did Guru Dutt produce the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa was produced under Guru Dutt's own banner, Guru Dutt Films.

questions.q: On which prestigious international list, recognizing the all-time greatest films, did Pyaasa later appear?
options: The Cannes Palme d'Or winners list
The Academy Award Best Picture list
The Berlin Golden Bear winners list
questions.explanation: Pyaasa was included in Time magazine's All-Time 100 Movies list.

questions.q: In the 1957 film Pyaasa, who profits from publishing Vijay's poetry after he is believed dead?
options: A publisher and Vijay's own brothers
questions.explanation: The publisher Mr. Ghosh and Vijay's brothers cash in on his poems once he is presumed dead.

questions.q: Guru Dutt's 1957 film Pyaasa is best described as belonging to which genre?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa is a social drama critiquing materialism and the neglect of artists.

questions.q: Which romantic duet, picturized on Guru Dutt and Mala Sinha, features in the dream and flashback scenes of the 1957 film Pyaasa?
options: Aaj Sajan Mohe Ang Laga Lo
Hum Aapki Aankhon Mein Is Dil Ko Basa Le To
questions.explanation: 'Hum Aapki Aankhon Mein' is the dream-duet recalling Vijay's college romance with Meena.

questions.q: The character Gulabo in the 1957 film Pyaasa cherishes Vijay's discarded poems because she believes in his what?
questions.explanation: Gulabo treasures Vijay's poetry, recognizing his talent when the world ignores him.

questions.q: The famous final image of Vijay framed in a doorway with arms outstretched in the 1957 film Pyaasa is often compared to which religious imagery?
questions.explanation: The backlit silhouette of Vijay in the doorway evokes a Christ-like crucifixion pose.

questions.q: Which playback singer voiced the song 'Aaj Sajan Mohe Ang Laga Lo' in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Geeta Dutt sang 'Aaj Sajan Mohe Ang Laga Lo' for Pyaasa.

questions.q: In which decade was Guru Dutt's acclaimed film Pyaasa released?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa was released in 1957, during the 1950s.

questions.q: The 1957 film Pyaasa is frequently cited alongside which other Guru Dutt film as part of his celebrated body of work?
questions.explanation: Kaagaz Ke Phool (1959) is another landmark Guru Dutt film often paired with Pyaasa.

questions.q: Which playback singer voiced the reflective song 'Jaane Woh Kaise Log The Jinke Pyaar Ko Pyaar Mila' in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Hemant Kumar sang 'Jaane Woh Kaise Log The' in Pyaasa, even though most of Vijay's other songs were sung by Mohammed Rafi.

questions.q: What kind of poetry does Vijay write in the 1957 film Pyaasa?
options: Sensitive verse on love and social injustice
questions.explanation: Vijay writes sensitive Urdu-Hindi verse dealing with love, loss and social injustice.

questions.q: What does Vijay ultimately reject at the end of the 1957 film Pyaasa once he is acclaimed as a great poet?
options: The fame and the corrupt society that ignored him in life
questions.explanation: Vijay disowns the hollow fame and the hypocritical society that valued him only when presumed dead.

questions.q: Pyaasa is widely regarded as a landmark of which strand of 1950s Hindi cinema that blended commercial form with serious social themes?
options: The socially conscious melodrama of Hindi cinema's golden age
questions.explanation: Pyaasa is a key work of the socially conscious melodrama that defined Hindi cinema's golden age of the 1950s.
